title: Frontend Frameworks Best Practices Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of theme consistency, accessibility (WCAG/Aria), and component composition for maintainability.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the primarily mistake developers make when working within a framework's design system?
options: Using ad-hoc custom styles (e.g., margin-top: 17px) instead of sticking strictly to the standardized theme spacings/colors.
To hide the JavaScript code from the console.

questions.question: Which and/or what role do 'Aria Labels' (e.g., aria-label) play in a professional frontend application?
options: They provide necessary descriptive information for users who use screen readers.

questions.question: Is accessibility (Aria, Keyboard Nav, Color Contrast) an optional feature for professional web apps?
options: No, it is a core web standard and essential for inclusive user experiences.
Yes, it is only for government websites.

questions.question: The pattern of encapsulating a long set of utility classes into a single reusable component (like <AppButton />) is called ________.
Does following a strict theme scale (spacing, colors, etc.) help maintain a consistent and professional brand identity?
options: Yes, as it prevents 'design drift' where different parts of the app look slightly different.
